### Import Pie Component and Stories from Recharts

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/polar/Pie.mdx

Imports the Pie component stories and Storybook documentation blocks for rendering interactive component documentation. Uses Storybook's Meta, Canvas, and Controls components to display the Pie component API and interactive controls.

```javascript
import { Meta, Canvas, Controls } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as ComponentStories from './Pie.stories';
```

--------------------------------

### Display Pie Component Storybook Documentation

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/polar/Pie.mdx

Sets up Storybook documentation for the Pie component using Meta, Canvas, and Controls. The Canvas displays the API story with padded layout, and Controls renders interactive property controls for the component.

```jsx
<Meta of={ComponentStories} />

<Canvas of={ComponentStories.API} layout='padded'/>

<Controls of={ComponentStories.API} />
```

--------------------------------

### Cell Component - Individual Element Customization

Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt

Customizes individual elements (bars, pie slices) with unique colors and interactive properties. The Cell component maps over data entries within Bar or Pie chart children, enabling per-element styling based on data properties. Supports onClick handlers and dynamic color assignment through helper functions.

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { BarChart, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Cell, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts';

const data = [
  { name: 'Product A', value: 400, status: 'success' },
  { name: 'Product B', value: 300, status: 'warning' },
  { name: 'Product C', value: 200, status: 'error' },
  { name: 'Product D', value: 278, status: 'success' },
  { name: 'Product E', value: 189, status: 'warning' },
];

const getColorByStatus = (status) => {
  switch (status) {
    case 'success': return '#00C49F';
    case 'warning': return '#FFBB28';
    case 'error': return '#FF8042';
    default: return '#8884d8';
  }
};

function CellExample() {
  return (
    <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={400}>
      <BarChart data={data} margin={{ top: 20, right: 30, left: 20, bottom: 5 }}>
        <CartesianGrid strokeDasharray="3 3" />
        <XAxis dataKey="name" />
        <YAxis />
        <Tooltip />
        <Bar dataKey="value">
          {data.map((entry, index) => (
            <Cell
              key={`cell-${index}`}
              fill={getColorByStatus(entry.status)}
              onClick={() => console.log(`Clicked ${entry.name}`)}
            />
          ))}
        </Bar>
      </BarChart>
    </ResponsiveContainer>
  );
}

export default CellExample;
```

--------------------------------

### Cell Component Usage in Recharts

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/component/Cell.mdx

Cell wrapper component specification for use within Pie, Bar, RadialBar, Scatter, or Funnel components. Cell allows specification of individual child attributes with props that have higher priority than parent component data attributes.

```jsx
// Cell can be wrapped by Pie, Bar, or RadialBar
// In Pie, for example, specify attributes of each child node through Cell props
// Cell props have higher priority than parent component data attributes
<Bar>
  <Cell />
</Bar>

<Pie>
  <Cell />
</Pie>

<RadialBar>
  <Cell />
</RadialBar>

<Scatter>
  <Cell />
</Scatter>

<Funnel>
  <Cell />
</Funnel>
```

--------------------------------

### Render PieChart Canvas in Storybook

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/PieChart.mdx

Sets up the Storybook Meta component for PieChart documentation and renders the Simple story example in a Canvas with padded layout. This creates an interactive preview of the PieChart component.

```jsx
<Meta of={PieChartStories} />

<Canvas of={PieChartStories.Simple} layout='padded'/>
```

### PieChart Component Hierarchy

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/PieChart.mdx

Documents the valid parent and child component relationships for PieChart in Recharts. PieChart must be wrapped in ResponsiveContainer and supports multiple child components for customization.

```jsx
<ResponsiveContainer>
  <PieChart>
    <PolarAngleAxis />
    <PolarRadiusAxis />
    <PolarGrid />
    <Legend />
    <Tooltip />
    <Pie />
    <Customized />
  </PieChart>
</ResponsiveContainer>
```

--------------------------------

### Import PieChart and Storybook Components

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/PieChart.mdx

Imports necessary Storybook documentation components and PieChart story definitions for rendering interactive documentation. Demonstrates the typical setup for documenting Recharts components using Storybook's addon-docs.

```javascript
import { Canvas, Meta, ArgTypes } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as PieChartStories from './PieChart.stories';
```

--------------------------------

### Render a Recharts PieChart with custom labels and interactivity in React

Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt

This React component demonstrates how to create a Recharts PieChart. It features custom percentage labels positioned dynamically, interactive slices that enlarge on hover, and a legend. It requires 'recharts' and 'react' for state management and rendering. The data is defined as an array of objects with 'name' and 'value' properties.

```jsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { PieChart, Pie, Cell, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts';

const data = [
  { name: 'Group A', value: 400 },
  { name: 'Group B', value: 300 },
  { name: 'Group C', value: 300 },
  { name: 'Group D', value: 200 },
  { name: 'Group E', value: 278 },
];

const COLORS = ['#0088FE', '#00C49F', '#FFBB28', '#FF8042', '#8884d8'];

const renderCustomLabel = ({ cx, cy, midAngle, innerRadius, outerRadius, percent }) => {
  const RADIAN = Math.PI / 180;
  const radius = innerRadius + (outerRadius - innerRadius) * 0.5;
  const x = cx + radius * Math.cos(-midAngle * RADIAN);
  const y = cy + radius * Math.sin(-midAngle * RADIAN);

  return (
    <text
      x={x}
      y={y}
      fill="white"
      textAnchor={x > cx ? 'start' : 'end'}
      dominantBaseline="central"
    >
      {`${(percent * 100).toFixed(0)}%`}
    </text>
  );
};

function PieChartExample() {
  const [activeIndex, setActiveIndex] = useState(0);

  const onPieEnter = (_, index) => {
    setActiveIndex(index);
  };

  return (
    <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={400}>
      <PieChart>
        <Pie
          data={data}
          cx="50%"
          cy="50%"
          labelLine={false}
          label={renderCustomLabel}
          outerRadius={120}
          innerRadius={60}
          fill="#8884d8"
          dataKey="value"
          activeIndex={activeIndex}
          activeShape={{
            outerRadius: 130,
            stroke: '#000',
            strokeWidth: 2,
          }}
          onMouseEnter={onPieEnter}
        >
          {data.map((entry, index) => (
            <Cell key={`cell-${index}`} fill={COLORS[index % COLORS.length]} />
          ))}
        </Pie>
        <Tooltip />
        <Legend />
      </PieChart>
    </ResponsiveContainer>
  );
}

export default PieChartExample;
```

--------------------------------

### Define AnimationTiming Type with JSDoc Comments for Auto-Generated Documentation in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/README.md

Demonstrates how to document TypeScript types using JSDoc comments that can be automatically extracted for Storybook documentation. The comment describes the purpose of the type, which defines easing function options for animations. This pattern enables auto-generation of arg descriptions in the Storybook API documentation.

```typescript
/** The type of easing function to use for animations */
export type AnimationTiming = 'ease' | 'ease-in' | 'ease-out' | 'ease-in-out' | 'linear';
```

--------------------------------

### ComposedChart Component Structure

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/ComposedChart.mdx

The ComposedChart component is a container for composed chart visualizations that can display multiple data series with different chart types simultaneously. It requires a ResponsiveContainer parent and supports multiple child components for customization.

```APIDOC
## ComposedChart Component

### Description
A flexible charting component that combines multiple chart types (Area, Line, Bar) in a single visualization. Used for displaying complex data with multiple dimensions and series.

### Component Hierarchy

#### Parent Component
- **ResponsiveContainer** - Required wrapper component that provides responsive sizing

#### Child Components (Supported)
- **XAxis** - Horizontal axis component
- **YAxis** - Vertical axis component
- **CartesianGrid** - Grid lines for better readability
- **Tooltip** - Interactive data point information display
- **Legend** - Chart legend for data series identification
- **Area** - Area chart data series
- **Line** - Line chart data series
- **Bar** - Bar chart data series
- **ReferenceArea** - Highlighted area reference zone
- **ReferenceLine** - Horizontal or vertical reference line
- **ReferenceDot** - Reference point marker
- **Brush** - Data range selector component
- **Customized** - Custom shape components
- **Valid SVG Elements** - Additional SVG elements for custom rendering

### Properties
Refer to ArgTypes documentation for complete property listing. Note: Properties in the 'Other' and 'Internal' groups are not recommended for use.

### Basic Usage Example
```jsx
import { ComposedChart, Area, Line, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts';

const data = [
  { name: 'Jan', uv: 4000, pv: 2400, amt: 2400 },
  { name: 'Feb', uv: 3000, pv: 1398, amt: 2210 }
];

<ResponsiveContainer width={800} height={400}>
  <ComposedChart data={data}>
    <CartesianGrid strokeDasharray="3 3" />
    <XAxis dataKey="name" />
    <YAxis />
    <Tooltip />
    <Legend />
    <Area type="monotone" dataKey="amt" fill="#8884d8" stroke="#82ca9d" />
    <Line type="monotone" dataKey="uv" stroke="#ffc658" />
    <Bar dataKey="pv" barSize={20} fill="#413ea0" />
  </ComposedChart>
</ResponsiveContainer>
```
```

--------------------------------

### Use `usePlotArea` Hook in Recharts Components

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/hooks/usePlotArea.mdx

This JSX code demonstrates how to import and integrate the `usePlotArea` hook within a functional React component using Recharts. The hook, when called, provides an object containing the pixel dimensions (width, height) and the top-left corner coordinates (x, y) of the chart's plot area. These values can then be used for custom rendering logic or positioning elements relative to the plot area.

```jsx
import { usePlotArea } from 'recharts';

function MyChart() {
  const plotArea = usePlotArea();
  // plotArea will contain { x, y, width, height }
  // Example: console.log(plotArea.width);
  return; // Render chart or custom components based on plotArea
}
```

--------------------------------

### Import Trapezoid with Storybook Documentation Setup

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/shapes/Trapezoid.mdx

Imports necessary Storybook addon modules and Trapezoid component stories for documentation and interactive testing. This setup enables the Meta, Canvas, and Controls components to display component API documentation and interactive controls in Storybook.

```javascript
import { Meta, Canvas, Controls } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as ComponentStories from './Trapezoid.stories';
```

--------------------------------

### Run Omnidoc Documentation Consistency Tests

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/omnidoc/README.md

Execute automated tests to verify documentation synchronization across TypeScript source code, API documentation, and Storybook. This command ensures consistency within the Recharts project's documentation by checking for discrepancies.

```shell
npm run test-omnidoc
```

--------------------------------

### ResponsiveContainer with initialDimension for Server-Side Rendering (SSR)

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/ResponsiveContainer.mdx

This JSX example illustrates using `ResponsiveContainer` with the `initialDimension` prop to provide fallback dimensions for server-side rendering (SSR) environments. This ensures the chart renders with specified dimensions (e.g., 520x400) before client-side hydration, preventing content layout shifts while waiting for browser API calculations to determine actual dimensions.

```jsx
<ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={400} initialDimension={{ width: 520, height: 400 }}>
  <LineChart {...args}>
    <Line dataKey="uv" />
  </LineChart>
</ResponsiveContainer>
```

--------------------------------

### FunnelChart with Child Components

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/FunnelChart.mdx

FunnelChart supports multiple child components including Funnel for data visualization, Legend for displaying series information, Tooltip for interactive data display, Customized for custom rendering, and valid SVG elements for additional graphical elements.

```jsx
<FunnelChart>
  <Funnel />
  <Legend />
  <Tooltip />
  <Customized />
</FunnelChart>
```

--------------------------------

### Import Storybook Documentation and Stories (JavaScript/MDX)

Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/ScatterChart.mdx

This code imports essential components for rendering documentation within Storybook's MDX format, including Canvas, Meta, and ArgTypes. It also imports all defined stories from './ScatterChart.stories' to power the interactive examples and property tables.

```javascript
import { Canvas, Meta, ArgTypes } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as ScatterChartStories from './ScatterChart.stories';
```
